analysis.name: sixpack
analysis.type: Nucleic Gene finding, Nucleic Translation, Display
analysis.description: Display a DNA sequence with 6-frame translation and ORFs

base.name: table
base.help: Genetics code used for the translation

list_item.shown_as: Mold, Protozoan, Coelenterate Mitochondrial and Mycoplasma/Spiroplasma

base.name: firstorf
base.prompt: ORF at the beginning of the sequence
base.help: Count the beginning of a sequence as a possible ORF, even if it is inferior to the minimal ORF size.

base.name: lastorf
base.prompt: ORF at the end of the sequence
base.help: Count the end of a sequence as a possible ORF, even if it is not finishing with a STOP, or inferior to the minimal ORF size.

base.name: mstart
base.help: Displays only ORFs starting with an M.

base.name: reverse
base.help: Display also the translation of the DNA sequence in the 3 reverse frames

base.name: orfminsize
base.help: Minimum protein size of Open Reading Frames (ORFs) to display in the translations.

base.name: uppercase
base.help: Regions to put in uppercase. If this is left blank, then the sequence case is left alone. A set of regions is specified by a set of pairs of positions. The positions are integers. They are separated by any non-digit, non-alpha character. Examples of region specifications are: 24-45, 56-78 1:45, 67=99;765..888 1,5,8,10,23,45,57,99

base.name: highlight
base.prompt: Regions to colour in HTML (eg: 4-57 red 78-94 green)
base.help: Regions to colour if formatting for HTML. If this is left blank, then the sequence is left alone. A set of regions is specified by a set of pairs of positions. The positions are integers. They are followed by any valid HTML font colour. Examples of region specifications are: 24-45 blue 56-78 orange 1-100 green 120-156 red A file of ranges to colour (one range per line) can be specified as '@filename'.

base.name: number
base.help: Number the sequence at the beginning and the end of each line.

base.name: width
base.help: Number of nucleotides displayed on each line

base.name: length
base.prompt: Line length of page (0 for indefinite)

base.name: name
base.help: Set this to be false if you do not wish to display the ID name of the sequence.

base.name: description
base.help: Set this to be false if you do not wish to display the description of the sequence.

base.name: offset
base.prompt: Offset to start numbering the sequence from
base.help: Number from which you want the DNA sequence to be numbered.
